message:
fix: harden encoding overflow checks, deterministic hash ring, and float test exactness

Distributed encoding: calculate_params now returns Result, with explicit
u16 overflow checks on source/repair symbol counts and u64 overflow check
on object size, replacing silent truncating casts. Redundancy ratio
computation corrected to widen each u16 to f32 before addition to avoid
intermediate overflow.

Consistent hash ring: switched internal node storage from HashSet to
BTreeSet so the nodes() iterator returns deterministic sorted order
regardless of hash seed, with a new test verifying the invariant.

TCP listener: flattened nested register_interest scoping — the
RearmDecision enum and match are now at the same level as the lock
acquisition, reducing rightward drift without changing behavior.

CLI progress: percentage clamped to [0, 100] before computing the bar
fill width, preventing underflow/overflow on out-of-range values.
New test covers the >100% edge case.

Latency algebra: documentation updated from "provable bounds" to
"model-based estimates" and added a numerical approximation note,
accurately reflecting that discrete breakpoint sampling is not a
formal proof object.

Database tests (MySQL, Postgres, SQLite, E2E): float literal test values
changed from inexact decimals (3.14, 2.718) to exactly representable
values (3.5, 2.5) to eliminate floating-point comparison brittleness.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
